article_title: Aurora Innovation Surpasses 370,000 Driverless Miles and Prepares to Deploy 200 Autonomous Trucks by Year-End
summary: Well-sourced Q1 2026 earnings report on Aurora Innovation; all facts corroborated; allowlist warnings are pipeline config issues, not editorial concerns

content_preview.title: Aurora Innovation Surpasses 370,000 Driverless Miles and Prepares to Deploy 200 Autonomous Trucks by Year-End
content_preview.summary: Aurora's Q1 2026 results show 370,000 driverless miles with zero attributed collisions, a second-gen hardware kit launching in Q2, and a 200-truck Sun Belt deployment target for year-end.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Aurora Innovation reported its first-quarter 2026 results on May 6, disclosing that its autonomous freight system had surpassed 370,000 driverless miles on U.S. public roads with zero attributed collisions and 100% on-time performance, according to [The Motley Fool's earnings transcript](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2026/05/07/aurora-aur-q1-2026-earnings-transcript/). The company is on the cusp of launching a second-generation hardware kit in Q2 and is targeting mo...

recommendations: Consider adding fool.com, stocktitan.net, ir.aurora.tech, investing.com, and tickerreport.com to config/source_allowlist.txt — these are established financial/IR outlets already used in published articles.
The investing.com source (source-1) was bot-blocked (403); all claims attributed to it are fully corroborated by the other four sources.

editor_notes.content_quality: Well-structured Q1 2026 earnings report following the standard News format (Overview, What We Know, What We Don't Know, Context). Technical depth is appropriate for an AV industry audience. The Motley Fool earnings transcript, Aurora's official IR press release, StockTitan press release, and Ticker Report call highlights form a solid corroborating evidence base. The internal comparison to the May 19 China autonomous trucking article adds useful context without editorializing.
editor_notes.source_verification: Four of five source snapshots successfully read: source-0.html.gz (Motley Fool Q1 2026 transcript, HTTP 200), source-2.html.gz (StockTitan press release, HTTP 200), source-3.html.gz (Aurora IR Fort Worth-El Paso expansion press release from October 2025, HTTP 200), source-4.html.gz (Ticker Report Q1 call highlights, HTTP 200). Source-1 (investing.com) was bot-blocked (HTTP 403) — no snapshot available. Every specific claim attributed to investing.com was independently verified against sources 0, 2, 3, and 4: Werner's 4,000+/week utilization, 12 routes, FirstLight 1km range and 34-second reaction time, Hirschbach 500-truck MOU, TaaS pricing $1.50-$2.00/mile, DaaS ~$0.85/mile, 15% fuel efficiency, Volvo VNL on Dallas-OKC, Q1 liquidity ~$1.3B, and capex/gross margin breakeven figures all confirmed. The Motley Fool transcript (source-0) misrenders 'New Braunfels' as 'Brownfields' — this is a transcript OCR artifact; Ticker Report (source-4) and the article correctly state 'New Braunfels, Texas.' All 5 source domains are credible: fool.com (Motley Fool earnings transcripts), investing.com (major financial data platform), stocktitan.net (PR aggregator used in prior published articles), ir.aurora.tech (official Aurora investor relations), tickerreport.com (financial call highlights). None are in source_allowlist.txt, but fool.com and stocktitan.net have been used in previously published articles without issue.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: All numerical claims verified against source snapshots: 370,000 driverless miles as of April (source-0), zero attributed collisions and 100% on-time (source-0), 12 routes (sources 0, 2, 4), seven customers (sources 0, 2, 4), FirstLight 1km range double nearest FMCW competitor (sources 0, 4), 34+ seconds reaction at highway speed (sources 0, 4), >50% hardware cost reduction vs gen-1 (sources 0, 2, 4), 1 million miles reliability target (sources 0, 2, 3, 4), Roush 20 trucks/week by Q3 (source-4), 1,000 trucks/year initial capacity (source-4), Hirschbach 500-truck MOU for DaaS (sources 0, 2, 4), TaaS $1.50-$2.00/mile (sources 0, 4), DaaS ~$0.85/mile (source-4), Q1 revenue $1M +10% sequentially (sources 0, 4), FY2026 guidance $14M-$16M +400% YoY (sources 0, 4), Q1 operating loss $244M incl $46M SBC (sources 0, 4), R&D $159M (sources 0, 4), liquidity ~$1.3B (sources 0, 4), 2026 peak capex ~$150M (source-4), gross margin breakeven at ~$80M TaaS run rate (sources 0, 4). The article's ~8x scale-up framing ('roughly eight-fold increase from approximately 25 trucks') is accurately sourced to CEO Urmson's direct statement from the call per Ticker Report. All CEO quotes checked verbatim — the two attributed quotes ('2026 is the year Aurora begins to scale' and 'We are hitting a new gear...') appear verbatim in source-0 and source-2 respectively.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and professional throughout. No editorializing, no loaded language. Forward-looking statements are properly qualified with 'targeting,' 'planning,' and 'expected.' The China comparison in the Context section is factual and directly references prior reporting without bias.
editor_notes.originality: No existing Aurora Innovation article found in the archive. Prior related coverage: an April 2026 autonomous trucking expansion article and the May 19 China self-driving truck comparison article. This Q1 2026 earnings report is a genuinely new story and not duplicative of any existing content.
recommendations: Add fool.com, stocktitan.net, ir.aurora.tech, investing.com, and tickerreport.com to config/source_allowlist.txt — these are established financial/IR outlets already used in published articles.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: High-quality submission covering Aurora Innovation's Q1 2026 earnings with thoroughness, accurate sourcing, and appropriate structure. The article is factually accurate, well-verified, and covers a genuine news event (milestone operational metrics and scale-up plans). Pipeline warnings about the allowlist are a configuration gap, not an editorial defect. Ready to publish as-is.
